title: Module 1
content:
Introduction to Finite Element Methods-Areas of Application-General steps
in Finite Element Methods- Geometric modelling techniques. Derivation of
the stiffness matrix using displacement technique- Spring, Bar, and truss
elements – Assembly of global stiffness matrix, boundary conditions.
Development of Euler beam equations- Finite element formulations.

title: Module 2
content:
Coordinate transformation- Global and local coordinate system, Interpolation
functions for general element formulation. Stiffness Matrix of Isoparametric
Elements.
Patch test, Mesh convergence – Basic concept only. Different type of
refinements (h, p and r).
Rayleigh’s- Ritz Method (Variational method) and weighted residual method.
Numerical integration using Gaussian quadrature.

title: Module 3
content:
Development of the Plane Stress and Plane Strain Stiffness Equations: -
Development of the CST, Linear-Strain Triangle (Shape functions and
equations only), Galerkin weighted residual Techniques, Types of Boundary
conditions (Neumann, Dirichlet and Robin)

title: Module 4
content:
Introduction to plate bending– finite element analysis of thin plate – finite
element analysis of thick plate –
Structural Dynamics, Mass matrix computation, Evaluation of eigen
values and eigen vectors, Transient analysis: Euler’s method, Central
difference technique, Critical time step, Rigid body modes.
